comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DEPLOY ATTESTATION (2026-07-13): autolander DEPLOYED + LIVE on the Gerrit host (i-00880b2c) — container healthy/0-restarts, flock+heartbeat+recovery in gerrit_autolander volume, autoheal sidecar, status endpoint via nginx (200 JSON), SSM token, CloudWatch alarm, Gerrit Autosubmit+rebase ACLs. Proven E2E: #618 landed by 'Rebar Bot Nava'.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Keep the single-instance bot alive under hangs, controllable in an incident, and — critically — **crash-safe**: a restart must never double-submit or strand a stack. This is the reliability-fallback backbone. Split out of S5 (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`).

## Files
- `infra/autolander/loop.py` — EDIT: heartbeat write, emergency-stop sentinel check, SIGTERM drain + recovery-record write, restart-recovery reconciliation. (`loop.py` is created NEW by the **S2** core-loop tasks — S5d `depends_on` **S2a** so the file exists before these lifecycle hooks are added.)
- `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` — EDIT: add the `HEALTHCHECK` line (the file itself is created NEW by sibling **S5a**; this task `depends_on` S5a so the create precedes this edit).
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` — EDIT: set `stop_grace_period: 120s` on the autolander service (so the SIGTERM drain can write `recovery.json`).
- `infra/autolander/tests/test_crash_recovery.py` — NEW: the crash-recovery/idempotency test suite (kill-at-each-phase + the drain⇄autoheal-race case).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Heartbeat (AC5):** the bot writes a timestamp file in the state volume once per its 15 s poll (S2 cadence); its age is served as `heartbeat_age_s` by S5c's status endpoint. Contract: freshness ≤ 15 s; `land`'s `lander_down` treats age **> 90 s** (6× poll) as down.
- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` marks the container `unhealthy` on a **hang** by stat-ing the **local heartbeat file** and failing if its age exceeds **90 s** (the `lander_down` bound) — it MUST NOT probe Gerrit (a Gerrit outage would flap restarts). **Concrete Docker parameters:** `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=60s --retries=2 CMD <stat-based check: unhealthy if heartbeat age > 90s>` (interval 30 s × retries 2 → ~90 s to trip, matching the bound; `start-period=60s` avoids boot false-positives). During the ≤120 s drain the bot keeps heartbeating each tick, so the age stays < 15 s and the container remains `healthy` — autoheal is not provoked. The `willfarrell/autoheal` sidecar (S5a) restarts the `autoheal=true`-labeled container on `unhealthy`.
- [ ] **Emergency-stop (prior art: Zuul `pause`):** an emergency-stop **sentinel file** in the state volume (presence = stop; operator creates to pause, removes to resume) transitions the bot to `paused` (the S2 `phase` value) — STOPS consuming new `Autosubmit` changes. Lives on the state volume so a redeploy does not silently un-pause.
- [ ] **Drain-on-shutdown + crash-safe recovery (RELIABILITY FALLBACK):** on SIGTERM the bot's **FIRST action is to write the recovery record** `recovery.json` to the state volume (the `wipChain` snapshot — `change_id`, `chain_member_ids`, `tested_shas`, `phase`, `re_drive_count`, and the S3 handback `acknowledged` flag) — written up-front so recovery is guaranteed no matter what interrupts the drain. It then attempts a graceful finish of an already-near-complete submit + S3 outcome write (prior art: Zuul `graceful`), time-bounded to **120 s**, with the compose service set to **`stop_grace_period: 120s`** so `docker compose stop/down` does not SIGKILL early.
- [ ] **Drain ⇄ autoheal race (must not fight each other):** during the drain the bot **keeps writing its heartbeat each tick** so the HEALTHCHECK stays healthy and autoheal is not provoked into a mid-drain `docker restart` (autoheal's `docker restart` does NOT honour `stop_grace_period`). And because `recovery.json` is written FIRST, even if autoheal *does* restart the bot mid-drain, recovery is still safe (the restart path below reconciles it). Belt-and-suspenders: no interruption can strand a stack.
- [ ] **Restart reconciliation** — on restart the bot reads `recovery.json` + reconciles against LIVE Gerrit and S3's markers BEFORE selecting new work, with a rule for every interruption point:
  - `phase=rebasing` — discard the in-memory `wipChain` and **re-select from scratch** (`Autosubmit` still set, Gerrit holds the current patchset). No submit happened; nothing to undo.
  - `phase=awaiting_verified` — discard in-memory state and **re-select**; the change is still `Autosubmit`+needs-fresh-`Verified`, so the loop re-enters `awaiting_verified` for the same patchset. Idempotent; no submit happened.
  - `phase=submitting` — check LIVE Gerrit: all members `MERGED` → write the `merged` outcome/ack; not merged → **re-drive**. Never blindly re-issue submit.
  - `phase=idle` — no in-flight work was captured; nothing to recover — proceed to normal selection.
  - `phase=paused` — recovery defers to the **emergency-stop sentinel file** (the source of truth, not the snapshot): if the sentinel is still present, stay `paused`; if it was removed, resume normal selection. (The `recovery.json` phase is advisory here; the sentinel decides.)
  - **an incomplete hand-back** (detected by an S3 marker with `acknowledged=false` — NOT a `wipChain` phase value) — complete the idempotent `Autosubmit`-removal across the stack, then set `acknowledged=true`.
  No double-submit; no stranded stack. (This covers all six `phase` values — `selecting` reduces to `idle`/re-select.)
- [ ] **Heartbeat-freshness proving test:** a test reads `heartbeat_age_s` from the S5c status endpoint (`GET /autolander/status`) while the bot runs and asserts it stays **< 15 s** (fresh); then it stops the heartbeat writer (simulating a hang) and asserts `heartbeat_age_s` crosses **90 s**, that the `HEALTHCHECK` flips the container to `unhealthy`, and that a concurrent `land` returns `lander_down` — bounds-checking the actual measured age, not just the prose contract.
- [ ] **Crash-recovery/idempotency test (the reliability guarantee):** an automated test kills the bot at each `wipChain` phase (`rebasing`, `awaiting_verified`, `submitting`) **and during an in-flight hand-back** (S3 marker `acknowledged=false`), and asserts the restarted instance neither double-submits nor strands a stack, and converges the S3 marker to `acknowledged=true`. A dedicated case asserts the **drain⇄autoheal race is benign**: a SIGTERM followed by an autoheal `docker restart` mid-drain still recovers cleanly (because `recovery.json` is written first).
compiled_state.title: S5d Liveness/heartbeat/autoheal + emergency-stop + drain + crash-safe recovery
